titleStmt.title: SII 16.319: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 319. (A.R. No. 376 of 1920.) ANUṀPALLI, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a rock behind the deserted Īśvara temple in the village.

The record is damaged. It is dated Śaka 1548 (current), Krōdhana, Phālguna ba. [30] corresponding to 1626 A.D., March 17, (Friday). The solar eclipse mentioned in the record occured a month earlier on Thursday, February 16.

It records a grant of land in Anuṁpalle on the occasion of a solar eclipse for lampofferings to god Yāgaṇṭīśvara by Vuyyāla Narasaṇḍu. The village Anumpalle was situated in Penakacharla-sīma in Jagatāpigutti-sīma.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . Avighnamastu .
svastiśrī jayābhyu
daya śālivāhana śakava¨rṣaṁbulu 1548 Agunēṭi krōdhana sa
ṁvatsara phālguṇa ba 30 lu śrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīra
rāmadēvarāyalayyavāru penugoṁḍa siṁhvāsanamaṁdu ratnasiṁhvāsanārūḍhuḍai
pṛthvisāmrājyaṁ sēyucuṁḍaṁgānu śrīmanmahānāyaṁkācārya
tōpili pedapāpināyani putṛṁḍaina yiṁmmaḍi pāpināyaḍu jagatāpigutti sī
maku cellē penakaca¨rlasīmalōni Anuṁpallegrāmāna vuyālavāhakuni
putṛlaina vuyāla narasaṁḍu vōbābhaṭlunigāru pratiṣṭha cēyiṁccina yāgaṁṭīśvaruni dīpārādhanaku
bhōnāna veṁpamāni cēnistaḷaṁ cēnu bījavari ¿1 . tū(ṁ)meṁḍuṁ nnirasa ma
māṁḍa varicēni Aṁccu nīṭikāluvastaḷaṁ guṁṭamaḍi bījavari ¿1 tū(ṁ)meṁḍuṁnnu
sūryagrahaṇa puṇyakālamaṁdu tamatallitaṁḍrādulku puṁṇyaṁgānu dhārabōsi yicina bhūdāna
dharma śilāśāsanaṁ yī dēvatāmānyānaku yavaru vakriṁcinanū tama tallitaṁḍrādu lanu vadhiṁcina pāpāna bōduru
gōhatya sisuhatya bṛhaṇahatya braṁhmahatya pātakālu sēsina pāpāna bōduru svadatā dviguṇaṁ pu
ṇyaṁ paradattānu pālanaṁ . paradattāpahārēṇa svadataṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēttani śrīsadāsivā¨rpa ṇama
stu mīśāvasēsi nityapūja bhōgakaṁveṭi yī cēnu maḍiṁni [Anubhaviṁpaṁ]
kalavāru
Anubhaviṁcaṁgalavāru Ani yiccina dharmaśāsanaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 16.319 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
